emails.subject: What Forum Health Denver does that your doctor hasn't tried
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

Most of our patients arrive at Forum Health Denver the same way.

They've had the standard labs. They've been told everything looks 'fine.' But [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] keeps getting worse — and nobody has looked deeper.

Dr. Fred Grover Jr. and the Forum Health team specialize in exactly that: looking deeper.

Here's what we offer that most doctors don't:
- Advanced diagnostics: DUTCH hormone panels, CIRS/mold testing, Cleerly heart scans, comprehensive metabolic mapping
- Membership-based care so you're never waiting months for a follow-up
- A care team that coordinates — not just a single rushed appointment
- GLP-1 weight loss programs with real medical supervision and lab tracking

**Forum Health Denver Care Options:**
- Initial Consultation: $595 (one-time)
- Peak Performance Membership: $195/month
- Advanced Wellness Concierge: $375–$500/month

If you've been searching for a doctor who will actually run the right tests and build a plan around your results — this is that practice.

We're accepting new patients in Denver.

emails.subject: Why CIRS is the most missed diagnosis in conventional medicine
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome — CIRS — is triggered by exposure to biotoxins, most commonly water-damaged building mold.

The biology is specific: biotoxins bind to receptors in the innate immune system, triggering a cascade of inflammation that conventional labs often fail to detect because standard panels don't look for it.

Symptoms include fatigue that sleep doesn't fix, brain fog, body aches, unusual sensitivity to light, and cognitive impairment. Sound familiar?

At Forum Health Denver, Dr. Grover uses the Shoemaker Protocol — the gold standard for CIRS diagnosis and treatment — combined with advanced testing including HLA-DR genetic susceptibility markers, C4a, TGF-beta-1, and visual contrast sensitivity testing.

Because [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] without an identifiable cause isn't random. There's a biological explanation — and it's findable.

If you've been dismissed by conventional medicine, an advanced chronic illness evaluation may be the missing piece.

emails.subject: Three years of specialists — then one visit changed everything
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

A patient — we'll call him R. — spent three years bouncing between specialists for fatigue, cognitive decline, and joint pain that had no explanation.

His labs were 'normal.' He was told to try antidepressants. He was dismissed more than once.

When R. came to Forum Health Denver, Dr. Grover ordered a comprehensive biotoxin panel. The results showed significant CIRS markers consistent with mold exposure from a water-damaged office building R. had worked in for years — an exposure he hadn't even connected to his symptoms.

Through the Shoemaker Protocol, targeted supplementation, and ongoing membership care, R. described his recovery over the following year as 'like coming back from the dead.'

Results vary — every patient's biology and circumstances are different, and no outcome can be guaranteed. But R.'s case represents what's possible when a practice finally runs the right tests.

If [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] has gone unexplained for too long, a comprehensive evaluation at Forum Health Denver may be your next step.

We're here when you're ready.
